vulnerability.hypothesis: In IsObjectEscaped, the MSlots case (line 287-304) only validates that MSlots consumers are StoreDynamicSlot/LoadDynamicSlot/StoreDynamicSlotFromOffset/LoadDynamicSlotFromOffset in DEBUG builds via MOZ_ASSERT. In release builds, if an MSlots node has an unexpected consumer (e.g. from a new MIR opcode or optimization pass that wasn't anticipated), the assertion is skipped and the object is treated as non-escaped. This could allow scalar replacement to proceed incorrectly, removing the object allocation while its slots pointer is used by an unexpected instruction, leading to use-after-free or type confusion when that instruction reads/writes through the dangling slots pointer.
